response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled senior/staff-level individual contributor with solid distributed-systems and backend credentials, but he is structurally unqualified for this CTO role. He has never held an engineering management, director, or executive position, and there is no evidence of him scaling or leading engineering teams of any size. His AI and agentic-systems exposure amounts to six months as a Staff Software Engineer at re:cinq, working on MCP tooling — valuable experience, but nowhere near the production multi-agent architecture leadership this role demands. His fintech exposure at RIDE Capital was as a developer applying TDD and trunk-based development practices, not as an architect of credit, risk, or payment infrastructure. The gap between his demonstrated seniority and the executive scope of this role is too wide to bridge.
strengths: Deep distributed-systems and high-performance backend experience: Dunnhumby/Sociomantic microservices at millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency patterns
Hands-on exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing awareness of emerging agentic paradigms
Long, polyglot engineering career demonstrating adaptability across web, mobile, embedded, and event-driven platforms
concerns: Zero executive, director, or engineering management experience: all roles are individual-contributor titles (Senior/Staff Software Developer, Founder of a small OSS project)
AI and agentic-systems production experience is limited to six months as an IC; no evidence of designing, scaling, or operating multi-agent systems in high-stakes environments
Currently on a career gap (caregiving since October 2025) following a short six-month stint at re:cinq, which weakens the case for an executive hire requiring immediate, sustained leadership

key_factors.factor: No engineering leadership or team-scaling track record
Insufficient depth in production agentic AI and fintech architecture
Strong distributed-systems and high-performance backend background
